overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong and highly targeted social media presence focused on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. Across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon, he successfully engages with researchers and academic communities. While LinkedIn provides the highest absolute engagement due to a larger follower base, Bluesky exhibits the highest relative engagement rate per follower, indicating a highly active and receptive audience on that decentralized platform.

contentThemes.description: Research and methodological discussions on tracking disinformation, information operations, and coordinated link-sharing campaigns.
Promoting post-doctoral positions and PhD opportunities within the research team to attract qualified computational social scientists.
Announcements of peer-reviewed papers and research tool updates, such as the release of CooRnet v2.0.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's highly active audience by posting more frequent, interactive research updates and thread-based methodological discussions.
Utilize visual assets (such as graphs, infographics, or screenshots of tool interfaces) on LinkedIn to further boost organic reach for job openings and grant announcements.
Cross-promote open-source software updates and academic vacancies across both Mastodon and Bluesky to bridge the decentralized academic community.

platformInsights.bluesky: Demonstrates outstanding relative engagement (averaging over 6% per post), making it the most responsive network per follower for publications and academic opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Provides a niche but dedicated technical audience, showing solid interest in tool updates like CooRnet v2.0, though with lower overall engagement volume.
platformInsights.linkedin: Serves as the primary driver for institutional reach, yielding the highest absolute volume of likes and comments, making it highly effective for job vacancies and funding announcements.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
